question: Compare the steam generator assembly (Figure 5.4-1) with the SG tube access view (Figure 5.4-2). How do they relate to maintenance?

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including low alloy steel for the baffle plate, various stainless steels, and nickel-based alloys for components such as the heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting, all in accordance with relevant BPVC specifications. It also references where additional material descriptions can be found in other sections of the report.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and share the vessel’s pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the reactor pressure vessel itself forming the shell and pressure boundary for the steam generators. The design ensures separation between the primary and secondary sides, with specific components forming the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document as needed for regulatory purposes, with all copies required to retain the copyright notice.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details the integration of the steam generators within the reactor vessel, the separation of primary and secondary sides, and references where further design specifics can be found in other report sections.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, and references related sections for further information on specific components and design evaluations.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) comply with relevant regulatory requirements, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria, by designing, fabricating, and testing reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Class 1 standards. It also clarifies the classification of connected piping and references the use of the 2013 Edition of the ASME BPVC, as endorsed by regulatory guides and rulemaking.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) editions as mandated by regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines compliance with regulatory requirements and ASME code cases for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details how the NuScale design adheres to specific editions and tables of the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and related Regulatory Guides, ensuring that all code cases used for design, fabrication, inspection, and testing meet NRC conditions. A consolidated list of applicable code cases is provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) requirements and relevant U.S. regulations to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance. These materials are subject to rigorous standards for design, fabrication, and testing, as well as quality assurance and fracture toughness criteria, to maintain the integrity and safety of the reactor coolant boundary. Material specifications for Class 1 components are detailed in Table 5.2-4.

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat following reactor shutdown, especially when normal cooling is unavailable, ensuring safe shutdown without external power. It maintains the reactor coolant system (RCS) temperature below 420°F within 36 hours to protect fuel and system integrity, and its reliability and risk significance are rigorously evaluated. The DHRS operates independently of the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS), but both can function together if needed.

edges.description: Section B references Section A for material descriptions relevant to the pressurizer, establishing a structural link where B relies on A for detailed information about reactor coolant boundary materials, which is pertinent for understanding maintenance requirements for both the steam generator assembly and tube access.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines relevant to the quality assurance and material reliability of steam generator components, while Section B describes the design and configuration of the steam generators within the reactor coolant system, connecting them through the regulatory and technical requirements that inform maintenance practices.

Section A lists references cited throughout the report, while Section B provides the preface and copyright information for the document, with Section A potentially referencing the document introduced in Section B.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that inform the quality assurance and maintenance requirements for reactor coolant boundary components, while Section B describes the physical design and configuration of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, which must comply with those referenced standards during maintenance activities.

Section B provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel and its components, including the steam generators, while Section A specifies material details for the pressurizer, which is also a component of the RPV described in Section B.

Section A provides specific references to detailed component descriptions within the reactor coolant system, while Section B serves as the general preface to the entire chapter containing those details, establishing the structural context for Section A.

Section A provides detailed material specifications for pressurizer components that are generally described as part of the reactor pressure vessel assembly in Section B, establishing a specification relationship between the two sections.

Section A provides technical details about the steam generator assembly referenced in Figure 5.4-1, while Section B is a preface that establishes the context and copyright for the entire chapter, including both figures, thus Section B frames the regulatory and documentation context for the maintenance-related information presented in Section A.

Section A (steam generator assembly) provides the overall configuration and placement of the steam generators, while Section B (SG tube access view) details the specific access points for maintenance activities, with the assembly view contextualizing the access view's relevance to maintenance procedures.

Section A discusses the design features that maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B lists references (such as EPRI and ASME guidelines) that provide supporting standards and guidelines for ensuring this integrity, including aspects relevant to maintenance.
Section B references Section A by citing Section 5.2 for material descriptions relevant to the pressurizer, which is part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary discussed in Section A.

Section B identifies the steam generators as pressure-retaining components within the reactor coolant system, which Section A states must maintain integrity as part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary for plant safety and maintenance considerations.

Section A states that the reactor coolant pressure boundary components, including the steam generator, comply with ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B lists references such as ASME NQA-1 and EPRI guidelines that provide the detailed standards and quality assurance criteria supporting this compliance.
Section B references Section A by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions relevant to the pressurizer, establishing a structural connection for maintenance considerations.

Section A establishes that the steam generator assembly, as part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, must comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, while Section B describes the physical integration and components of the steam generators within the reactor system, connecting their design and accessibility to maintenance considerations.

Section A describes the use and material requirements of threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B lists references (such as ASME and EPRI guidelines) that provide the standards and guidelines these fasteners must comply with, directly connecting the design and maintenance practices to established regulatory and industry requirements.

Section A specifies the material and inspection requirements for threaded fasteners used in reactor coolant pressure boundary components, while Section B references the materials used in pressurizer components, both of which are relevant to maintenance activities involving inspection and material compatibility.

Section B describes the overall design and components of the reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators relevant to maintenance, while Section A details the inspection and testing requirements for pressure boundary components (such as fasteners) that are part of or associated with the steam generator assembly, thus connecting the general system design to specific maintenance-related inspection practices.

Section A describes the design features that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B specifies that these features comply with regulatory codes and standards (10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC), directly connecting the design intent to regulatory compliance.

Section A defines the scope and components of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B details the inspection and testing requirements for threaded fasteners within that boundary, connecting them through the need to maintain boundary integrity for safe maintenance.

Section A establishes that all reactor coolant pressure boundary components, including those relevant to the steam generator, comply with ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B specifies that the threaded fasteners used in these components (such as those in the steam generator assembly) also conform to ASME BPVC standards, thus both sections are connected through their adherence to the same regulatory codes which directly impact maintenance procedures and requirements.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME and NRC requirements for design, fabrication, and testing, while Section B lists reference documents (such as EPRI guidelines and ASME standards) that provide the technical basis for those requirements, connecting them through the regulatory and technical standards used in maintenance planning.

Section B references Section A for material descriptions of the pressurizer components, indicating that the material requirements and specifications in Section A are applicable to the components discussed in Section B.

Section A outlines regulatory compliance and code requirements for overpressure protection and inservice testing, which are prerequisites for the design and maintenance of steam generator components described in Section B.

Section A describes the regulatory and material standards required for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B lists references (such as ASME and EPRI guidelines) that Section A relies on to substantiate its compliance claims.
Section B references Section A by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are detailed in Section A.

Section A specifies the material requirements and regulatory compliance for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which includes the steam generator tubes discussed in Section B as part of the reactor pressure vessel assembly relevant to maintenance activities.

Section A references the use of ASME BPVC codes and regulatory requirements for overpressure protection, while Section B specifies that the reactor coolant pressure boundary materials comply with those same ASME BPVC codes and regulations, connecting them through shared compliance standards.

Section A describes the design and function of the Decay Heat Removal System, which relies on the integrity and maintenance of components like the steam generator, while Section B lists references including quality assurance and material reliability guidelines that inform maintenance practices for such components.

Section B describes the physical integration of the steam generators and the decay heat removal system within the reactor coolant system, while Section A details the functional role of the decay heat removal system, which relies on the steam generators for heat transfer during maintenance-related shutdowns.
